assessments.rationale: Product is certified to EN 179 and EN 1125; OSHA and NFPA 101 govern US warehouses. Compliance is a product feature, not a barrier.
Mechanical-primary architecture pre-satisfies the absolute constraint. Power outages during warehouse operations are a known scenario; fail-safe design is a selling feature.

Large DCs (20,000-100,000+ m2) place emergency exits 100-500 m from the nearest gateway. Steel racking and tilt-up concrete walls significantly attenuate 868 MHz. Repeater infrastructure resolves this.
assessments.mitigation: Pre-deployment RF propagation survey; Deploy wireless repeater chain (one stage per ~30 m); Optimize gateway placement at racking-aisle intersections

assessments.rationale: Dock exits see frequent activation during shift changes and truck arrivals. Risk of sub-2 year battery life is real but manageable with proactive PM scheduling.
assessments.mitigation: Deploy battery monitoring variant; integrate low-battery alert into WMS/CMMS; Establish quarterly PM sweep for high-activation dock exits

assessments.rationale: Industrial warehouse environment has no aesthetic or tamper-resistance requirements that conflict with surface-mount design.
Large DCs use BACnet/IP BMS. EasyWave does not natively interoperate, but the GfS BUS gateway provides dry contact outputs compatible with any BMS input.
assessments.mitigation: Install GfS Wireless BUS Interface; Map dry contact outputs to BMS input points; Include BMS integration scope in project specification

assessments.rationale: At 100 exits, hardware cost is ~EUR 73K vs. ~EUR 35K for mechanical-only. Gap requires ROI justification. 3PLs have structured procurement; labor savings provide credible ROI case.
assessments.mitigation: Build TCO model; Negotiate volume pricing for 3PL portfolio deployments; Phase deployment starting with highest-risk dock exits

assessments.rationale: EU warehouse operators employ large hourly workforces; door timestamps may be attributable to individuals via shift correlation. GDPR mitigable via data minimization.
assessments.mitigation: Configure zone-level event logging; Conduct legitimate interest assessment for safety-critical monitoring; Execute DPA with monitoring SaaS provider

assessments.rationale: Standard dry-storage aisles pose no IP risk. Loading dock exits and cold-storage exits require IP45 variant (catalog item).
assessments.mitigation: Specify IP45 variant for dock doors and cold-chain exits; Conduct exit-environment survey at commissioning; For freezer exits at -25C: verify electronics operating range

assessments.rationale: Standard warehouse deployment is standalone Türwächter — no powered locking component. EN 13637 does not apply to typical warehouse use case.
